claims.text: The GAA c.1935C>A variant was the most frequent mutation observed in the study cohort, present in four out of ten Chinese patients with infantile-onset Pompe disease.

citations.quote: The most frequent mutation observed in our study was c.1935C > A, with four out of ten patients having this mutation.

claims.text: Patient 0103 (female, Han, onset age 4 months, age at first infusion 5.8 months) carried c.1935C>A p.Asp645Glu (het) compound heterozygous with c.2662G>T p.Glu888* (het), was CRIM-positive, with GAA activity 1.05 μmol/L/h, LVMI 291.1 g/m2, LVEF 61.1%, and no ventilator use at baseline.

Patient 0104 (female, She ethnicity, onset age 6 months, age at first infusion 7 months) carried c.1432G>A p.Gly478Arg (het) compound heterozygous with c.1935C>A p.Asp645Glu (het), was CRIM-positive, with GAA activity 0.37 μmol/L/h, LVMI 745.5 g/m2, LVEF 49.2%, and no ventilator use at baseline.

Patient 0105 (female, Han, onset age 5 months, age at first infusion 5.9 months) carried c.1832G>A p.Gly611Asp (het) compound heterozygous with c.1935C>A p.Asp645Glu (het), was CRIM-positive, with GAA activity 1.10 μmol/L/h, LVMI 298.8 g/m2, LVEF 41.1%, and no ventilator use at baseline.

Patient 0302 (male, Han, onset age 4 months, age at first infusion 5.4 months) carried c.1935C>A p.Asp645Glu (het) compound heterozygous with c.2662G>T p.Glu888* (het), was CRIM-positive, with GAA activity 6.8 nmol/g/min, LVMI 361.9 g/m2, LVEF 79.8%, and no ventilator use at baseline.

Patient 0401 (male, Han, onset age 6 months, age at first infusion 7.4 months) carried c.1121G>A p.Cys374Tyr (het) compound heterozygous with c.1935C>A p.Asp645Glu (het), was CRIM-positive, with GAA activity 2.4 nmol/h/mgPr, LVMI 231.8 g/m2, LVEF 44.0%, and no ventilator use at baseline.

All patients carrying c.1935C>A were CRIM-positive (each had at least one missense mutation), as the study protocol designated CRIM-positive status to any patient carrying at least one missense mutation.

citations.quote: If a patient had at least one missense mutation, he or she was considered CRIM-positive.

claims.text: All 10 patients in the study, including those carrying c.1935C>A, had classic infantile-onset Pompe disease characterized by onset before 12 months of age, left ventricular hypertrophy (LVM Z scores >10 at baseline), and GAA enzyme activity below the lower limit of normal.

citations.quote: All subjects had left ventricular mass (LVM) Z scores >10 at baseline, and eight subjects (80%) achieved Z scores <5 at week 52.

claims.text: The pathogenicity of GAA mutations in this study, including c.1935C>A, was determined according to the Pompe disease GAA variant database.

citations.quote: The pathogenicity of mutations was determined according to the Pompe disease GAA variant database (https://www.pompevariantdatabase.nl).
